decisions.title: Prioritize Sift Edge production readiness over feature expansion
decisions.context: Multiple Edge tickets (simulator, metrics, enum/bitfield support, WAL recovery, sync prototype) signal readiness push. Edge is a 2026 moat-builder. Need to decide: ship minimal viable Edge Q2 or wait for full feature set Q3.

arguments.for: Early Edge deployment captures telemetry decisions at source (North Star metric), validates moat thesis with real customers, faster feedback loop for Embed Deeper pillar
arguments.against: Incomplete feature set (enum/bitfield, WAL recovery) risks customer trust, support burden increases, may need to rebuild if architecture assumptions fail

decisions.title: Resolve performance bottleneck strategy: optimize existing APIs vs. API redesign
decisions.context: E1/E2 excessive calls to channel list/search + GetPhaseAnnotations latency issues. Channel list v2 in flight. Pattern suggests API design issue, not just implementation. Affects Explore V2 performance.
decisions.blockedBy: Need profiling data from production to quantify v1 vs v2 improvement and inform breaking change decision

arguments.for: V2 APIs with batching/pagination solve root cause, cleaner long-term architecture, aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar for Explore performance
arguments.against: Breaking changes slow customer adoption, engineering cost of dual API support, incremental optimization of v1 may be sufficient for 80% of cases

decisions.title: Establish calculated channel error handling policy
decisions.context: Two tickets (ENG-8965, ENG-9069) address different error scenarios for calculated channels. No consistent strategy: surface errors vs. graceful degradation vs. fail-fast. Python Rules reliability depends on this.

arguments.for: Surfacing errors increases transparency for rule authors (Signal>Evidence>Decision), enables self-service debugging, reduces support tickets
arguments.against: Error noise overwhelms users if not contextualized, graceful degradation maintains system availability, partial data better than no data for decisions

decisions.title: Deploy HA Keycloak to GovCloud now or wait for commercial cloud validation
decisions.context: ENG-7813 migrating GovCloud to HA Keycloak. GovCloud customers are high-value but small user base. Risk/reward calculation different than commercial. Auth downtime = zero decisions captured.

arguments.for: GovCloud customers expect higher availability SLAs, HA Keycloak already validated in staging, delay risks competitive displacement in government contracts
arguments.against: Small user base means lower validation, GovCloud-specific issues hard to debug, wait 2 weeks for commercial cloud deployment learnings

decisions.title: CVE remediation scope: patch immediately vs. bundled security release
decisions.context: Three CVEs identified (apache-avro RCE, zookeeper auth bypass, crypto/tls). RCE is critical but requires specific attack scenario. Patching breaks kluctl deployment workflow currently in progress.
decisions.blockedBy: Risk assessment incomplete - need exploitability analysis for Sift's specific deployment architecture

arguments.for: RCE vulnerability requires immediate patch regardless of exploitability likelihood, demonstrates security-first culture, regulatory compliance for GovCloud
arguments.against: Disrupts active deployment work (kluctl), no evidence of active exploitation, bundled release in 5 days allows proper testing and rollback plan

keyResults.name: Explore V2 Performance: P95 query latency <2s

timeAudit.weekSummary: Engineering team fragmented across 30+ active tickets spanning Edge (moat), performance (core), security (hygiene). No clear sequencing signal. Edge has 8 parallel workstreams—risk of shipping nothing complete.
alerts: Zero focus time scheduled Thu/Fri - all-hands and sprint planning collision
5 critical decisions pending with <1 week runway - decision debt accumulating
Performance work (E1, E2, GetPhaseAnnotations) has no unified owner - diffusion of responsibility
Security CVEs aging 3+ weeks - need dedicated remediation sprint or accept risk explicitly

events.title: CVE Remediation Decision Gate
events.description: Go/no-go on immediate patching vs bundled release. Blocks kluctl deployment work.
